title: Comparative Literature
description: Reading across cultures and eras: tradition, influence, world-literature perspective.
summary_pt_br: Literatura comparada: tradição, influência, world literature, tradução (= interpretação).

scenes.subtitle_en: Reading across cultures and eras: tradition, influence, world-literature perspective.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Literatura comparada: tradição, influência, world literature, tradução (= interpretação).

scenes.text_en: Reading across cultures and eras: tradition, influence, world-literature perspective. Focus: comparative literature. Borges and Calvino share a playfulness that crosses languages.
scenes.text_pt: Literatura comparada: tradição, influência, world literature, tradução (= interpretação). Foco: comparative literature. Borges e Calvino compartilham leveza que atravessa línguas.

scenes.title: Comparative Literature
scenes.body_en: Key ideas: TRADITION (what a work inherits), INFLUENCE (direct lineage), WORLD LITERATURE (works that travel beyond origin), TRANSLATION (always an interpretation).
scenes.body_pt: Literatura comparada: tradição, influência, world literature, tradução (= interpretação).

examples.en: Borges and Calvino share a playfulness that crosses languages.
examples.pt: Borges e Calvino compartilham leveza que atravessa línguas.

examples.en: World literature names works that cross their original contexts.
examples.pt: World literature = obras que cruzam seus contextos.

examples.en: Every translation is an interpretation wearing editorial modesty.
examples.pt: Toda tradução é interpretação com modéstia editorial.

exercise.question: Complete: "Borges and Calvino share a ___ that crosses languages."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "Borges and Calvino share a playfulness that crosses languages."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Borges e Calvino compartilham leveza que atravessa línguas.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I haven't heard anything about that.
World literature names works that cross their original contexts.
exercise.explanation: Correct: "World literature names works that cross their original contexts."
exercise.hint_pt_br: World literature = obras que cruzam seus contextos.

exercise.explanation: Key terms from the lesson — match each to its meaning.

scenes.title: Comparative Literature — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre comparative literature. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Name two writers that share tradition across languages.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Sound and idiom always, sense sometimes.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Can you read influence without reading the predecessor?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Literature is the longest conversation humans have kept alive.

scenes.title: The comparative seminar
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, análise, e sua opinião crítica. O professor avaliará.
scenes.prompt_en: Summarize two ideas from comparative literature. Use at least three terms.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Análise crítica com quem falou, argumentos, e sua reflexão.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: In the comparative literature seminar, Kumar walked students through Borges, Calvino, and the line that connects them.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: World literature names works that travel beyond their original context without losing their essential force.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: Borges and Calvino share a playfulness with form that survives every translation into every new language.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: Every translation is an interpretation wearing editorial modesty — a second author quietly beside the first.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: And canon is less a ranking than a shared vocabulary that lets readers across centuries talk to each other.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Literature, Kumar believed, is the longest continuous conversation humans have ever managed to keep alive in writing.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the advanced class just finished "Comparative Literature". Reading across cultures and eras: tradition, influence, world-literature perspective. Borges and Calvino share a playfulness that crosses languages. World literature names works that cross their original contexts. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that at this level, reading widely and writing daily are non-negotiable.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is non-negotiable at this level?
check.explanation: At C-level, wide reading and daily writing are essential.
check.hint_pt_br: Leitura ampla e escrita diária são essenciais no C.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in advanced class we studied "Comparative Literature". It was demanding but genuinely rewarding. See you next session!

scenes.title: Go deeper — advanced resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes avançadas pra aprofundar. Leitura regular é o que separa um aluno avançado de um proficiente.
